title: prunus serotina subsp. capuli
intro: Prunus serotina subsp. capuli, also known as the Mexican cherry or capulí, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Prunus serotina subsp. capuli include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
